title: No schema-write Pi tool — in-pi agents cannot define new block kinds or evolve existing schemas
description: There is no registered Pi tool to define a new block kind's schema or evolve an existing one. read-schema is the only schema tool (read-only). writeSchema / updateSchema / readSchema exist with AJV meta-validation (schema-write.ts) plus a per-schema migration registry (schema-migrations.ts), but have no pi.registerTool twin. So a tools-only in-pi agent cannot extend the substrate's type surface — every new block kind or schema version must be authored through the orchestrator CLI or by hand. project-init scaffolds only the legacy defaults/ schema set; there is no tool path to add a schema beyond it.

evidence.reference: only read-schema registered; no write-schema / update-schema tool
writeSchema/updateSchema/readSchema library surface with AJV meta-validation — no Pi-tool twin
per-schema version-to-version migration registry exists; a schema-evolution tool must honor it (version bump + MigrationFn registration)
DEC-0019/0020 (dual-surface) + DEC-0023 (schema title/display_name) + schema-versioning ($id + version + migration) — Pi-tool twin missing

impact: New block-kind definition and schema evolution are orchestrator-CLI-only; in-pi agents and tool-only consumers cannot extend or migrate the type surface. Onboarding a custom block kind (DEC-0038) and any in-pi schema authoring are blocked. Completes the trio of write-twin dual-surface gaps with FGAP-075 (edge-write) + FGAP-076 (config-write). Lower frequency than edge/config operations (hence P2) but required for full tool-side substrate self-definition.
proposed_resolution: Register schema-write Pi tool(s) — the dual-surface twin of schema-write.ts (write-schema + update-schema) — with AJV meta-validation and migration-registry awareness (version bump + MigrationFn registration on evolution). Outcome-only; survey the pi-sdk + sibling pi extensions for the canonical shape. DEC-0019/0020 + DEC-0023 + schema-versioning.
